question: Are organic foods significantly safer and more nutritious than conventional foods?
topic: Research shows minimal nutritional differences and pesticide residues below safety thresholds on con

sources.name: Sustainable Diets, Food, and Nutrition: Proceedings of a Workshop

discovery_sources.text: EWG Dirty Dozen list causes unnecessary alarm about pesticide residues that are 'far below any health risk safety threshold' - consistent with EPA/FDA positions on pesticide safety
discovery_sources.origin: Unbiased Science: The Joy of Cooking (For Kids Who Are ‘Picky’ Eaters)

discovery_sources.text: Organic pesticides can require higher application rates and have greater environmental impact than some conventional alternatives - claim needs verification against NASEM pesticide reports
discovery_sources.origin: Unbiased Science: The Joy of Cooking (For Kids Who Are ‘Picky’ Eaters)

discovery_sources.text: Mere presence of pesticide residue does not equate to harm - aligns with standard toxicology principles
discovery_sources.origin: Unbiased Science: The Joy of Cooking (For Kids Who Are ‘Picky’ Eaters)

discovery_sources.text: Pesticide residues on conventional vs. organic produce
